skill.name: reporting-sprints
skill.description: Delegates sprint report creation to the @sprint-report-generator agent.
skill.summary: Create sprint reports by routing the task to a dedicated sprint reporting agent.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3, but that line only contains the skill description. No cryptographic code, prompt injection, network access, file access, or command execution evidence was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is plain metadata: a description for sprint reporting. No weak cryptographic algorithm or unsafe cryptographic behavior is present at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line contains only descriptive text for the skill. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, code block, or executable instruction on the flagged line.

content.value_statement: Sprint reporting can take time when updates are scattered across work items and notes. This skill routes the task to a sprint report generator so teams can produce focused status summaries.
actual_capabilities: Routes sprint report requests to the @sprint-report-generator agent.
Frames the assistant task around sprint reporting.
Supports concise reporting workflows for agile teams.
Works as a lightweight skill with no scripts or bundled tools.
limitations: Requires the @sprint-report-generator agent to be available in the environment.
Does not define a report template or required sprint metrics.
Does not fetch project data from external systems by itself.
Does not include validation rules for report accuracy.

use_cases.title: Prepare Team Sprint Updates
use_cases.description: Create a clear summary of completed work, blockers, and next steps for a sprint review.

use_cases.title: Summarize Delivery Progress
use_cases.description: Turn sprint notes into a concise progress update for stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Document Engineering Status
use_cases.description: Generate a structured sprint report from engineering updates and issue notes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Sprint Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sprint report from these notes: [paste sprint notes]. Include completed work, blockers, and next steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have simple notes and need a quick report.

prompt_templates.title: Stakeholder Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a stakeholder-ready sprint report from this sprint data: [paste data]. Keep it concise and focus on outcomes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when leaders need a short status update.

prompt_templates.title: Team Retrospective Input
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sprint report that highlights delivery results, unresolved blockers, quality concerns, and retrospective discussion points.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a retrospective or sprint review.

prompt_templates.title: Executive Delivery Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an executive sprint report from these updates: [paste updates]. Include progress against goals, risks, decisions needed, and next sprint priorities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when reporting delivery status to senior stakeholders.

output_examples.input: Sprint notes with completed tickets, two unresolved blockers, and planned work for the next sprint.
output_examples.output: A concise sprint report with sections for completed work, blocked items, risks, and next sprint priorities.

output_examples.input: Raw updates from engineers, product, and QA at the end of a sprint.
output_examples.output: A stakeholder-friendly summary that separates outcomes, issues, quality notes, and follow-up actions.

output_examples.input: A sprint goal, burndown notes, and a list of carryover items.
output_examples.output: A delivery-focused report that explains progress against the goal and identifies carryover risks.

best_practices: Provide completed work, blockers, risks, and next sprint plans in the prompt.
Specify the intended audience before generating the report.
Review the final report against source notes before sharing it.
anti_patterns: Do not use incomplete notes as the only source for a final status report.
Do not ask for unsupported integrations unless another tool provides the data.
Do not publish generated reports without checking sensitive project details.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to use the @sprint-report-generator agent to create a sprint report.

faq.question: Does it connect to Jira or GitHub?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not include any integration or data connector.

faq.question: Can it create reports for executives?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide the sprint details and ask for an executive-focused format.

faq.question: Does it define a standard sprint report template?
faq.answer: No. The skill only delegates report creation to the named agent.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains no confirmed dangerous behavior or prompt injection.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
